notes:
Opening opportunity work notes, 2026-06-29T23:27Z.

Record inspection: wait_for_opportunity returned openings:plaintiff, final action record_opening_statement, text limit 5000, target first submission <=3750. Used get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence, read_evidence_range. Visible record has one case-packet evidence item only: ev_54607018b65c_market-rules, market-rules.md, sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6, 1650 bytes, system case packet, juror visible. No party evidence, exhibits, reports, arguments, rebuttals, or closings yet.

Packet contents: market question asks whether Israel invaded Syria in 2024. Guidelines resolve Yes if Israel commenced a military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Syria from 2024-09-12 through 2024-12-31 23:59 ET; Golan Heights count as Israeli territory; resolution source can be official confirmation by Syria, Israel, UN, or any permanent UNSC member, or consensus credible reporting. Packet includes Polymarket Yes rationale: Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon on 2024-12-17 and announced Israeli forces would remain on the peak until another arrangement guarantees Israel security; credible reporting had previously confirmed Israeli forces reached the peak on 2024-12-08; rationale distinguishes Syrian peak of Mount Hermon from the Golan Heights. Analysis: packet proves rules and market rationale, but underlying facts should still be independently admitted later.

Source map: decisive elements are date window, Syrian geography outside market-counted Golan, military crossing/offensive, intent to establish control, and authority/proof source. Strong primary targets: Israeli PMO/GPO/IDF statements or images from 2024-12-17; UN/UNDOF or UN spokesperson statements about IDF in area of separation from 2024-12; Security Council records with Syria/UN/P5 confirmations; credible reporting (AP/Reuters/Times of Israel/JPost) for sequence. Adverse theory: Israel called movement targeted/temporary/defensive after Assad collapse and Syrian troop abandonment; defense may argue no offensive or no intent to establish control, or that buffer zone/Hermon geography is ambiguous. Response theory: market does not require annexation or combat with Syrian army; establishing/fortifying/holding positions in Syrian territory for security control satisfies control even if styled temporary.

Native investigation performed for theory only, not record proof: web searches for Netanyahu quote, Dec. 8 Hermon reports, UN buffer-zone confirmations, official gov.il/UN sources. Leads found: AP 2024-12-17 article at https://apnews.com/article/syria-war-assad-israel-news-17-december-2024-4d7f0b35e08d5e853c776193272fb2d6 reports Netanyahu said Israeli forces would stay in seized buffer zone until a security arrangement; comments were from summit of Mount Hermon inside Syria; Katz instructed IDF to establish presence and fortifications; UN spokesman said advance violates buffer-zone agreement. UN Security Council PDF S/PV.9857 at https://digitallibrary.un.org/record/4076069/files/S_PV.9857-EN.pdf contains later official confirmations: UNDOF says IDF established multiple positions in area of separation in violation of 1974 agreement; Syrian representative describes crossings, seizure of hundreds sq km around Quneitra/Jabal al-Shaykh, posts/helipad. UN India page https://india.un.org/en/286171-syria includes statement that what UN was seeing was violation of 1974 disengagement agreement. AP 2024-12-10 lead reports Israeli troops began moving into buffer zone Saturday and included Syrian side of Mount Hermon, with Israeli official framing as targeted temporary control. Need submit these sources as evidence in later evidence-admission phase before relying on them as support.

Persistent journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with inspected evidence, source URLs, search queries, adverse checks, theory, and unresolved gaps.

Opening plan: address council; explain current record is thin and what it proves/does not prove; identify real dispute and plaintiff theory; emphasize future proof targets; avoid citing unadmitted outside sources as proof.

Defendant opening work notes for arb-ex04-20260629232350.

Record/opportunity: defendant opening, opportunity_id openings:defendant. get_case/list_evidence show one case-packet evidence item, ev_54607018b65c_market-rules, sha256 54607018b65c81f4921890ad041c7d94def6c581d10571fdc1c8100c80c5a1e6, size 1650, markdown, juror_visible, case_packet; no submitted outside evidence yet. read_evidence_range offset 0 length 1650 completed. Plaintiff opening is in record.

Case-packet contents: Polymarket market question and guidelines; Golan Heights count as Israeli territory; resolution requires Israeli military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Syria between 2024-09-12 and 2024-12-31 23:59 ET. Resolution source: official confirmation by Syria, Israel, UN, or permanent UNSC member, or consensus credible reporting. Packet says Polymarket resolved Yes because Netanyahu visited Syrian side of Mount Hermon on Dec. 17 and announced Israeli forces would remain on the peak until another arrangement guaranteeing Israel security; it says reporting previously confirmed forces reached peak Dec. 8; it says Syrian peak of Mount Hermon is not part of Golan Heights.

Source map: decisive elements are timing, geography outside market-defined Golan, conduct as military offensive, and intent to establish control. Best plaintiff source targets: UNDOF press statement, Israeli PMO/GPO Dec. 17 statement/video/photo, credible AP/PBS/Reuters/Times of Israel reporting. Best defense/adverse checks: official Israeli statements framing move as temporary/defensive in abandoned buffer after Assad collapse; whether action was an offensive or emergency deployment; maps/legal status of area of separation and Mount Hermon.

Native investigation performed for opening (not admitted, for theory only): web searches for Netanyahu Mount Hermon Dec. 17 2024, UNDOF Dec. 13 IDF movements area of separation, official gov.il/PMO sources, temporary defensive Israeli framing. Leads found: UNDOF press statement pages on peacekeeping.un.org and undof.unmissions.org apparently state since Dec. 7 UNDOF observed significant increase in IDF movements within area of separation and along ceasefire line and as of Dec. 13 IDF remained in area of separation in multiple locations. Media leads report Netanyahu on Syrian side of Mount Hermon and statement that forces remain until another arrangement; several sources also frame Israel's move as limited/temporary/defensive after Assad's fall. Official PMO/gov.il page not located in quick gov.il search; may need official social/video/GPO or archived copies later.

Opening strategy: Do not rely on outside sources as record proof. Acknowledge case packet establishes rules and resolution rationale but not underlying source chain. Defense theory: plaintiff must prove more than a temporary defensive buffer-zone deployment; the words offensive and intended to establish control matter. Later evidence must supply precise maps, official statements, UNDOF text, timeline, and source-chain context. If admitted sources show Syrian-side seizure and hold with control intent, defense will have to confront that; current record has not yet done so.

Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search log, URLs/leads, and unresolved gaps.

Merits argument work notes, plaintiff, 2026-06-29T23:33Z.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff, final action submit_argument, text limit 6000, up to 9 exhibits, evidence admission allowed. Start-of-turn record rescan completed with get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence. Record had market-rules case packet only as evidence; both openings present. Defendant opening focused on source-chain precision: exact place, whether outside Golan Heights, temporary/defensive deployment vs offensive/control, official/source details.

Evidence plan: submit targeted source captures for (1) date/window commencement, (2) Syrian geography/Mount Hermon/buffer zone, (3) control/intent, (4) official UN/Syria/P5 confirmation, and (5) adverse context of temporary/defensive framing. Avoid duplicative articles.

Evidence submitted and accepted:
1. ev_c87bdd160b3a_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-c87bdd160b3a. AP 2024-12-17 report, URL https://apnews.com/article/syria-war-assad-israel-news-17-december-2024-4d7f0b35e08d5e853c776193272fb2d6. Captured excerpts: Netanyahu said Israeli forces would stay in seized buffer zone until security arrangement; he entered Syrian territory; comments from summit of Mount Hermon inside Syria about 10 km from Israel-held Golan; Israel seized swath of southern Syria and captured roughly 400 sq km demilitarized area in Syrian territory; Katz instructed IDF to establish presence and fortifications for possible extended stay; UN spokesman said advance violates deal and occupation remains occupation. Weight: credible secondary reporting with GPO/AP image caption and direct reported statements; strong for Dec. 17 location/control/intent and UN response. Limitation: not the original Israeli PMO page.
2. ev_e049416d3ae0_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-e049416d3ae0. UN Security Council S/PV.9857 PDF excerpts, URL https://digitallibrary.un.org/record/4076069/files/S_PV.9857-EN.pdf. Captured official statements: UNDOF says IDF established multiple positions in area of separation in violation of 1974 agreement; A3+ condemns Israeli incursions into Syrian territory and presence in demilitarized buffer zone; Greece condemns declared indefinite presence; France calls on Israel to withdraw and respect Syria sovereignty; China urges withdrawal from Syrian territory; Syria says forces crossed lines, seized hundreds sq km in and around Quneitra and Jabal Al-Shaykh, built bases/posts/helipad. Weight: official UN/Security Council and Syria/P5 confirmation. Limitation: Feb. 2025 transcript, so use with AP and packet for within-window commencement.
3. ev_b0349334e954_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-b0349334e954. AP Dec. 2024 spotlight, URL https://www.ap.org/news-highlights/spotlights/2024/as-israel-advances-on-a-syrian-buffer-zone-it-sees-peril-and-opportunity/. Captured: troops began moving into buffer zone Saturday; included Syrian side of Mount Hermon; Israeli official Saar said IDF took targeted and temporary control near border; Netanyahu said Israel would occupy summit. Weight: strong for commencement timing and adverse framing that still concedes control. Limitation: secondary source/excerpt.
4. ev_9e078b6c01cd_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-9e078b6c01cd. UN India page, URL https://india.un.org/en/286171-syria. Captured Pedersen/UN-hosted remarks that UN message from New York was that what was being seen was violation of 1974 disengagement agreement. Weight: official corroboration; less detailed than Security Council record.

Persistent journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with evidence IDs, relevance, limits, and theory.

Argument plan: Offer market-rules packet as PX-0, AP Dec. 10 as PX-1, AP Dec. 17 as PX-2, UN Security Council as PX-3, UN corroboration as PX-4. Argue elements: date satisfied by AP Dec. 10; geography satisfied by Syrian side Mount Hermon/buffer zone in Syrian territory and packet distinction from Golan; military offensive/control satisfied by troops entering, seizing/capturing, occupying, fortifying, multiple positions; intent satisfied by targeted/temporary control, stay until arrangement, fortifications, later official record of bases/posts/helipad. Address adverse: temporary/defensive motive does not negate control; later UN record confirms rather than creates event; provenance is sufficient under preponderance though original Israeli PMO source would be even better.

Defendant merits argument notes for arb-ex04-20260629232350.

Opportunity: arguments:defendant. Record scanned with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. New plaintiff evidence: ev_c87bdd160b3a AP Netanyahu/buffer-zone report, ev_e049416d3ae0 UN Security Council transcript excerpts, ev_b0349334e954 AP buffer-zone/Mount Hermon report, ev_9e078b6c01cd UN India Pedersen remarks. Exact bytes read for all four with read_evidence_range. Plaintiff evidence is materially adverse: AP places Netanyahu on Mount Hermon inside Syria and quotes Israeli stay until security arrangement; AP captures 'targeted and temporary control'; UN transcript confirms IDF multiple positions in area of separation and Syrian/P5 withdrawal demands.

Defense investigation: searched for official/credible temporary defensive framing. Queries included Gideon Saar targeted temporary control, gov.il Saar briefing Dec 9 2024, Israel Syria buffer zone temporary limited measure Reuters, Reuters Saar temporary buffer zone. Found gov.il official page for FM Sa'ar briefing but curl was Cloudflare-blocked; not submitted because exact content could not be captured beyond search-result lead. Found Reuters article reproduced by Arab News at https://www.arabnews.com/node/2582545/middle-east, opened/read. Key defense lines: sterile defense zone would be enforced without permanent Israeli presence; Shoshani said IDF not advancing toward Damascus, not involved in Syria internally, not a side in conflict, no interest other than protecting borders/citizens; article says Israel calls incursion temporary border-security measure. Mixed/adverse parts: acknowledges demilitarized zone inside Syria, Syrian side of Mount Hermon, abandoned Syrian military post, and some points outside separation area.

Submitted defense evidence: ev_cf16e8709259_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-cf16e8709259, Reuters/Arab News article, sha256 cf16e87092598332ea0be39f387cdc3b4c5e8f731bbeb684275c276c1e2dfa75, size 2948, accepted as submitted evidence.

Merits theory: Full denial is difficult. Best defense is narrow textual reading: market requires military offensive intended to establish control; evidence also supports temporary, non-permanent, border-security move after Syrian position collapse rather than an offensive to establish territorial control. Attack February 2025 UN transcript for later timing and diplomatic/adversary characterizations, though it is official and weighty. Acknowledge that if council reads temporary buffer-zone seizure/security hold as enough, plaintiff likely meets burden. Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal work notes, 2026-06-29T23:38Z.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff, final actions submit_rebuttal or pass, text limit 4000, remaining submitted evidence 4, exhibits 7, reports 3. Record/evidence scan completed with list_evidence, stat_evidence for D-1 and PX-3, and read_evidence_range of D-1 exact bytes.

New defense material: ev_cf16e8709259_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-cf16e8709259, Reuters via Arab News, source URL https://www.arabnews.com/node/2582545/middle-east, title about Israel imposing a sterile defense zone in southern Syria with no permanent Israeli presence. Exact read showed: headline says Israel will impose sterile defense zone in southern Syria; subheadline says Israel denied significant penetration beyond zone; Katz said Israel ordered forces to create sterile defense zone in southern Syria enforced without permanent Israeli presence as Israel tightens its hold along line between Syria and Israeli-occupied Golan; Katz said zone would prevent establishment/organization of terror in Syria; military spokesperson said Israeli troops remained in demilitarized buffer zone in Syrian territory and a few additional points outside separation area; he said IDF not advancing toward Damascus and not a side in conflict; section labeled LIMITED, TEMPORARY MEASURE says Israeli troops moved into demilitarized zone inside Syria including Syrian side of strategic Mount Hermon overlooking Damascus, where it took over an abandoned Syrian military post; Reuters added uncertainty on how far beyond buffer zone troops had stopped and three security sources said Israelis advanced beyond demilitarized zone.

Rebuttal analysis: D-1 does not defeat plaintiff; it concedes core facts. 'Without permanent presence' is not same as 'without control.' The market requires intended control, not permanent annexation. A sterile defense zone in southern Syria enforced by Israel, tightening its hold, troops remaining in Syrian territory and additional points, and taking over a Syrian military post on Syrian Mount Hermon all support market-defined control. Temporary/defensive motive explains why Israel asserted control; it does not negate that control. D-1 should be offered as PX-R1 because it directly answers defense and is already visible.

No new outside evidence needed for rebuttal; existing admitted evidence and D-1 are enough. Persistent journal updated.

Defendant surrebuttal notes for arb-ex04-20260629232350.

Opportunity: surrebuttals:defendant. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Plaintiff filed a rebuttal but submitted no new source evidence; it reused defense evidence ev_cf16e8709259 as PX-R1 and filed a technical report analyzing D-1. Evidence list unchanged aside from already-admitted D-1. D-1 metadata checked again.

Plaintiff rebuttal strongest points: D-1 confirms Israeli troops in Syrian territory, a sterile defense zone, tightening hold, presence in a few points outside the separation area, the Syrian side of Mount Hermon, and takeover of an abandoned Syrian military post. Plaintiff argues temporary/defensive motive does not negate control.

Surrebuttal analysis: No new external source needed; dispute is interpretive. Best response is to concede D-1 proves presence and takeover of an abandoned post, but emphasize the market does not ask merely whether Israel controlled a point or violated the agreement. It asks whether Israel commenced a military offensive intended to establish control. D-1's limits remain relevant to intent: no permanent presence, no Damascus advance, not a side in Syrian internal conflict, no interest beyond protecting borders/citizens, temporary border-security purpose. Plaintiff's reading effectively collapses any temporary security control into the full market element and gives little independent meaning to 'offensive' and 'intended.' Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing work notes, 2026-06-29T23:40Z.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff, final action deliver_closing_statement, text limit 5000, no new evidence/reports. Start-of-closing record scan completed with get_case, list_evidence, stat_evidence for D-1. Visible record includes market packet, plaintiff AP and UN evidence, defendant Reuters/Arab News evidence, arguments, rebuttal, surrebuttal, and technical reports. No new evidence files after D-1.

Defendant surrebuttal: concedes D-1 confirms Israeli troops in demilitarized zone inside Syria, including Syrian side of Mount Hermon, and takeover of an abandoned Syrian military post. Defense says those facts prove presence/operational control but not 'military offensive intended to establish control' because qualifiers show temporary, defensive, no permanent presence, no advance to Damascus, no side in Syria internal conflict.

Closing analysis: decisive facts are now largely undisputed. PX-1 fixes December 2024 commencement and Syrian side of Mount Hermon; PX-2 fixes Netanyahu at Mount Hermon inside Syria, stay-until-arrangement, Katz fortifications; PX-3 official UN/Syria/P5 confirms multiple IDF positions in area of separation, Syrian account of crossings/seizure/posts/helipad, France/China withdrawal demands; D-1 confirms sterile defense zone in southern Syria, troops in Syrian territory plus additional points, and takeover of Syrian military post. Defendant's qualifiers go to motive/duration, not control. The market requires intended control over any portion of Syria, not permanent annexation, Damascus advance, governance, or participation in civil conflict. A military-imposed security zone inside Syria is control. The Golan carveout means the Syrian side of Mount Hermon is not excluded.

Source-weight audit: Original Israeli PMO/GPO source not captured/admitted; AP/Reuters are credible secondary captures and include official statements; official UN/Syria/P5 confirmations provide authority. The record is enough by preponderance.

Defendant closing notes for arb-ex04-20260629232350.

Opportunity: closings:defendant. wait_for_opportunity returned closing. Record inspected with get_case and list_evidence. Plaintiff closing is present. No new evidence was added after surrebuttal; evidence list unchanged. D-1 metadata had already been checked in surrebuttal and no new exact bytes were needed for closing because no new evidence was added.

Plaintiff closing crystallizes the case: facts largely undisputed; plaintiff argues temporary/security control is still control and the market does not require permanence, annexation, Damascus advance, or involvement in Syria's internal conflict.

Closing plan: Concede adverse record facts to preserve credibility: Syrian-side Mount Hermon/demilitarized zone, abandoned Syrian military post, multiple positions, and stay until security arrangement. Focus on proposition's limiting language: 'military offensive intended to establish control,' not merely presence, violation, occupation, or temporary security zone. Best defense facts: D-1 says no permanent presence, no Damascus advance, no involvement in Syria internally, not a side in the conflict, only interest border/citizen security; PX-1 says targeted temporary control; PX-4 only proves violation; PX-3 is later and includes diplomatic/adversarial statements. Ask for No or at least burden not met, while acknowledging broad reading favors plaintiff. Cross-turn journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
